requirements.title: Task Management API
requirements.purpose: A collaborative task management system that enables teams to create, assign, track, and discuss tasks in real-time.
requirements.scope: In-scope v1: REST API with JWT auth, CRUD tasks, comments, WebSocket notifications, RBAC. Out-of-scope: file attachments, calendar integration, Gantt charts.

functional_requirements.description: Users shall register with email and password, receive email verification.
Users shall authenticate via email/password and receive JWT tokens.
Users shall create tasks with title, description, priority, due date, and assignee.
Users shall update task status (todo, in_progress, done) and other fields.
Users shall delete tasks they own or have admin rights for.
Users shall assign tasks to other registered users.
Users shall add comments to tasks with markdown support.
Users shall receive real-time WebSocket notifications on task updates.
Users shall search tasks by title, status, priority, and assignee.
Admins shall manage user roles and permissions.

non_functional_requirements.description: API responses shall be under 200ms at 95th percentile under 500 RPM.
All passwords shall be hashed with bcrypt; all API traffic over TLS 1.3.
The system shall maintain 99.9% uptime during business hours.
The API shall support up to 10,000 concurrent users.
WebSocket reconnection shall happen within 3 seconds on disconnect.

user_stories.description: As a team member, I want to create and assign tasks so that work is organized.
As a manager, I want to view task progress in real-time so I can track project status.
As an admin, I want to manage user roles so that access control is maintained.

open_issues: Should we support OAuth2 as an alternative auth method?

architecture.title: Task Manager System Architecture
architecture.overview: Layered REST API architecture with WebSocket support for real-time updates. PostgreSQL for persistence, Redis for pub/sub messaging and caching.

components.name: API Gateway
components.description: Entry point for all client requests, handles auth and routing

components.name: Task Service
components.description: Core business logic for task CRUD operations

data_flow.description: Client authenticates via POST /auth/login, receives JWT
Client sends authenticated request to API Gateway
Gateway validates JWT, routes to Task Service
Task Service processes request, reads/writes PostgreSQL
Task Service publishes event to Redis pub/sub
Notification Service picks up event, pushes to connected WebSocket clients

architecture.deployment_strategy: Docker Compose for development. Kubernetes for production with HPA auto-scaling.
security_considerations: JWT with 15min access and 7-day refresh tokens
